skill.name: code-review-checklist
skill.description: A structured code review checklist for functionality, security, performance, testing, documentation, and maintainability.
skill.summary: Guide code reviewers through consistent, security-aware pull request reviews.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, credential, environment, network, and weak-cryptography patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown checklist examples, prose, or reference links, with no executable scripts, automatic network calls, credential access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: Markdown examples misclassified as command execution
low_findings.description: The backtick findings are fenced Markdown examples, JavaScript template literals in sample code, or review comment templates. The skill provides review guidance and does not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are inside Markdown fences or inline skill references. No runnable script, shell command, or instruction to invoke a command is present.

low_findings.title: Illustrative secret and environment examples
low_findings.description: The API key and process.env references appear in a security checklist example showing bad and good patterns. They are not used by the skill to read credentials or access environment files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The section labels the key as a bad example and immediately contrasts it with environment variable usage. There is no surrounding executable code path.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography findings are plain text
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography scanner hits are prose, headings, or URLs in a checklist document. No cryptographic function, hashing algorithm call, or implementation guidance for weak crypto was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of each line shows ordinary documentation text or a resource URL. No crypto API usage or security recommendation for weak algorithms exists.

low_findings.title: External links are reference resources
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are listed under Additional Resources. They do not cause network activity unless a user chooses to open the references.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are plain Markdown links to public code review and security guidance. The skill contains no fetch, curl, browser automation, or exfiltration instruction.

content.user_title: Review Code with a Practical Checklist
content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss defects when teams rely on memory alone. This skill provides a repeatable checklist for functionality, security, tests, performance, documentation, and review comments.
actual_capabilities: Breaks reviews into functionality, security, performance, tests, documentation, and Git checks.
Provides example issue patterns for validation, SQL injection, hardcoded secrets, and code clarity.
Supplies reusable review comment templates for change requests, questions, and positive feedback.
Helps teams define consistent pull request review standards.
Highlights common review pitfalls and practical mitigation steps.
limitations: Does not run tests, linters, scanners, or repository commands by itself.
Does not replace project-specific architecture or domain expertise.
Does not verify vulnerability data for dependencies automatically.
Requires reviewers to adapt the checklist to local standards.

use_cases.title: Standardize team pull request reviews
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to make review coverage consistent across functionality, tests, security, and documentation.

use_cases.title: Train new reviewers
use_cases.description: Give junior developers a structured review flow and examples of common issues to catch.

use_cases.title: Audit risky changes before merge
use_cases.description: Apply the security and performance sections before approving sensitive or high-impact code changes.

prompt_templates.title: Review a small pull request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review-checklist skill to review this pull request. Focus on functionality, tests, and clear feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Create a team review checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review-checklist skill to create a pull request checklist for our team. Include security, tests, performance, and documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Assess security-sensitive changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review-checklist skill to review these authentication and data handling changes. Prioritize validation, authorization, secrets, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Run a comprehensive release review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the code-review-checklist skill to perform a release readiness review. Cover functionality, regressions, performance risks, security concerns, tests, documentation, and review comments.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that adds a user registration endpoint.
output: Functionality: Check validation for email, password length, duplicate accounts, and failed database writes.
Security: Verify password hashing, rate limiting, error messages, and authorization boundaries.
Tests: Request coverage for invalid input, duplicate users, and successful registration.

output_examples.input: Create review feedback for a complex order processing function.
output: Issue: The function handles validation, payment, email, and inventory updates in one block.
Suggestion: Split the workflow into focused steps with clear error handling.
Why: Smaller units are easier to test, review, and maintain.

output_examples.input: Prepare a team checklist for pull request reviews.
output: Pre-review: Read the description, linked issue, and test plan.
Quality: Check naming, structure, duplication, and project conventions.
Merge readiness: Confirm tests, documentation, migrations, and compatibility notes.

best_practices: Use the checklist as a baseline and add project-specific requirements.
Review small changes first so feedback stays specific and complete.
Prioritize correctness, security, and tests before style comments.
anti_patterns: Do not approve changes without reading the implementation and tests.
Do not treat the checklist as a substitute for running project tools.
Do not give vague feedback when a concrete example would help.

faq.question: Does this skill run code or tests?
faq.answer: No. It provides a review checklist and guidance for the reviewer to apply.

faq.question: Can it find every security issue?
faq.answer: No. It highlights common security review areas, but it does not replace security testing.

faq.question: Is it useful for small pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. Reviewers can use the relevant sections and skip items that do not apply.

faq.question: Can teams customize the checklist?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed as a baseline for team-specific review standards.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The marketplace metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What review areas does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers functionality, security, performance, tests, documentation, Git hygiene, and review comments.
